name: Romanian Strigoi Lore
description: Romanian Strigoi Lore encompasses the beliefs and practices surrounding the Strigoi, troubled spirits of the dead believed to rise from their graves and torment the living. Originating from ancient Dacian and Slavic paganism, combined with later Christian influences, this lore focuses on identifying, preventing, and combating Strigoi. Methods include rituals performed at funerals to prevent becoming a Strigoi, such as piercing the heart with a stake, placing garlic in the coffin, or burying the body face down. Protective measures against Strigoi attacks involve the use of garlic, holy water, crosses, and specific incantations. Belief in Strigoi persists in rural areas, where traditional remedies and rituals are still practiced. Cultural significance resides in the fear and respect accorded to the dead and the importance of proper burial rites. The lore also includes stories of living Strigoi, individuals born with specific signs who are believed to possess vampiric or malevolent powers. These individuals are often ostracized or subjected to preventative measures during their lifetimes to neutralize their potential threat.
subtitle: Dealing with the restless dead.

keyMyths.title: The First Strigoi
keyMyths.description: A wicked man dies and returns as a Strigoi to torment his family. A brave villager uses a stake to stop him.

keyMyths.title: The Living Strigoi
keyMyths.description: A child born with a caul is destined to become a Strigoi. Rituals are performed to prevent this.

sources.author: Mircea Eliade
sources.description: Discussion of Dacian religious beliefs and their influence.
sources.text: Discussion of Dacian religious beliefs and their influence.
